title: Formation Pulse
problem: Formation Pulse has the highest segment-level onset risk: the current classic pulse is a bright synthetic stab while the Galaga cadence reference has more low-frequency body and softer attack.
strategy: Capture baseline, hand-designed candidates, and reference subclip windows through the live browser audio engine, compare against the canonical application-guide reference window, and promote only candidates that clear explicit keeper gates.
successMeasure: A keeper must improve whole-cue risk, onset segment risk, cadence pressure, and masking separation while preserving duration pocket, band shape, and segment-role guards.

referenceWindow.selection: highest-scoring non-overlapping reference subwindow after explicit Aurora audio-theme capture

candidates.label: Cadence generator anchor-body-sub-support

generator.target: Raise low-band body while reducing brightness, zero-crossing density, and RMS for formation pressure cadence.

axes.interpretation: The pressure cue should carry body without being mistaken for a hit or explosion.
A formation bed should sit below shot/hit/explosion loudness so momentary events remain readable.
The pulse must fit the repeated stage cadence pocket.
A calmer bed leaves sharper high-frequency texture for action cues.
Normalized brightness share is a secondary warning: the bed may be quiet, but it should still leave spectral space for action cues.
Formation cadence should keep actual bright-band loudness below combat events, not only reduce normalized spectral share.

onset.interpretation: onset timbre is the leading gap: band shape/rolloff differ from the reference segment.
